title: EMILIA PÉREZ
writer: Jacques Audiard in collaboration with Thomas Bidegain
logline: A lawyer helps a dangerous cartel boss fake his death and undergo gender transition surgery to become Emilia Pérez, but years later finds herself drawn into Emilia's new life as an advocate for the disappeared.
protagonist: Rita (the lawyer who facilitates the transformation)
antagonistOrConflict: The violent cartel world and the impossible moral complexity of Emilia's dual identity
tone: Dark, operatic, morally complex with moments of tenderness

actBreaks.description: End of Act 1: Manitas reveals his plan for transformation inside his trailer

validation.ruling: Scene #30 at 31.5% effectively concludes Act 1. Rita's completion of the family's escape plan (passports, accounts, Swiss house) while Manitas sings about his desires represents the end of the setup phase. This scene transitions from Rita's initial survival/cooperation to her deeper involvement in protecting the family, establishing the new status quo for Act 2.

actBreaks.description: End of Act 2: The discovery of bodies in the hangar marks the beginning of Emilia's reckoning with her past

validation.ruling: Scene #67 at 63.7% appropriately ends Act 2. The fundraiser gala where Emilia/Rita court corrupt officials and drug dealers while Rita voices moral objections represents the height of their compromised position. This scene escalates the central conflict about dirty money and sets up the final act's moral and physical confrontations.

actBreaks.description: Final scene: Resolution in Mexico City suburbs

validation.ruling: Scene #106 at 99.2% provides proper resolution. Epifanía's funeral procession with Emilia's statue and mourning song offers closure through community remembrance. The transformation of Emilia into a folk saint figure completes her character arc from cartel wife to protector of the disappeared, while the religious imagery provides thematic resolution about sacrifice and redemption.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: A transformation story that moves from criminal underworld through identity metamorphosis to moral reckoning
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Act 1 builds tension through Rita's mundane lawyer life to the shocking cartel encounter. Act 2 explores the aftermath of transformation with domestic scenes contrasted against violence. Act 3 accelerates toward violent confrontation and tragic resolution. Musical numbers punctuate emotional peaks throughout.

incitingIncident.description: Inside SUV, hooded Rita is violently thrown sideways when car hits bump. Hood slips enough for her to glimpse men's silhouettes, dashboard lights, and monotonous landscape rushing by.

validation.ruling: Scene #12 at 12.9% marks the clear inciting incident - Rita's kidnapping and transportation to the desert compound. This abduction launches the main narrative, transforming Rita from courthouse employee to unwilling participant in cartel operations. The escalating tension from scenes 11-15 (desert convoy, armed men, drone surveillance) confirms this as the catalyst that propels the story forward.

midpoint.description: Rita and Emilia visit a market where Rita reconnects with familiar shop owners. Emilia links arms with Rita and offers to pay her to stay, suggesting she do nothing but just be there.

validation.ruling: Scene #53 at 48.4% functions as an effective midpoint. Rita's encounter with the grieving mother distributing missing person flyers represents a crucial shift in perspective - from personal survival to understanding the broader human cost of disappearances. This revelation about the victims' families and Rita's growing awareness occurs at the perfect structural position and creates the emotional turning point that drives the second half.

climax.description: The supernatural smoke enters Emilia's mouth, awakening her. She reveals to Jessi that she is Manitas, her husband, confessing how she fell in love with Jessi through her sister years ago.

validation.ruling: Scene #98 at 94.4% serves as the emotional and narrative climax. During the gunfight, Emilia's dying confession about her past relationship with Jessi reveals the deepest character truth while under maximum physical danger. This scene combines the highest emotional stakes (love confession) with peak action (bullets piercing walls) and represents the convergence of all narrative threads in the final act.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Aerial establishing shot of Mexico City at night, shimmering lights like stars, with a distant peddler's chant about buying mattresses and appliances echoing through the urban landscape.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT MENDOZA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Camera approaches luxury apartment building, revealing silhouettes behind curtains. Extreme violence as a woman is attacked by a man who throws her over a balcony railing to her death. Image freezes in flash of light.
scenes.turningPointNote: Opens with shocking violence that sets the dark tone

scenes.heading: INT. 7-ELEVEN MARKET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita Mora Castro, 27, unremarkable in appearance, practices her legal speech in a 7-Eleven while surveillance footage plays on screens. She delivers her defense argument to no one listening.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN CHAPULTEPEC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita walks empty streets at night, repeating 'going up, going down' when a crowd suddenly appears behind her - dog walkers, vendors, demonstrators who overtake her as she continues her speech about violence, love and death.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN CHAPULTEPEC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita walks through crowded market accompanied by young women who repeat her words. She delivers passionate speech about bought justice, paid verdicts, and gossip running in the streets. Crowd joins in her refrain.

scenes.heading: INT. MEXICO CITY COURTROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In courtroom, Rita watches her boss Berlinger deliver her closing argument with theatrical poses while she mouths the words and discretely feeds him notes. The case involves Gabriel Mendoza.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Outside courthouse, Rita calls her mother about winning the case but admits having a bad taste in her mouth. Behind her, Mendoza and Berlinger bask in TV camera spotlight.

scenes.heading: INT. WOMEN’S RESTROOM COURTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: In courthouse women's restroom, Rita has blood on her hands. Her phone rings from unknown caller who knows she's in the bathroom and suggests she deserves the applause, not her boss.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mysterious caller knows Rita's location - first direct threat

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita follows cleaning ladies through courthouse corridors, beginning to sing about how long she'll keep her head down, lick boots, and work for nothing. Chorus voices ask what she has to gain.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEWSSTAND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At newsstand with assassin's pixelated face on tabloid covers, Rita continues singing with growing conviction that she has nothing to lose and everything to gain. Chorus voices echo her words.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHIHUAHUA DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Aerial shot of SUV speeding through Chihuahua desert at night, headlights cutting through darkness, leaving trail of dust while narco-corrido music plays on radio.

scenes.heading: INT. SUV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Inside SUV, hooded Rita is violently thrown sideways when car hits bump. Hood slips enough for her to glimpse men's silhouettes, dashboard lights, and monotonous landscape rushing by.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita kidnapped and transported to unknown location

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Through car window, Rita sees ruined bridge with corpses hanging from railings as they pass through the desert landscape.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: SUV dims lights and joins massive convoy of thirty trucks - pickups with machine guns, trailers, armored SUVs - all moving through the desert before stopping.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Still hooded, Rita is escorted through stopped convoy of armed, masked men. She sees wounded being treated, hears drone surveillance reports of enemies 30km away. Led up metal steps into Manitas' trailer.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita meets the powerful cartel boss Manitas Del Monte

scenes.heading: INT. BERLINGER’S OFFICES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Back in Berlinger's office, Rita sits at her desk as if working normally while Manitas' voice-over explains his need for a 'reset' in a safe place with untraceable, competent help.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUVARNABHUMI AIRPORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita arrives at Bangkok airport and meets Asian surgeon. She inquires about complete sex change operations, discussing mammoplasty, vaginoplasty, rhinoplasty, laryngoplasty as medical diagrams show surgical procedures.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita begins researching gender transition surgery for Manitas

scenes.heading: EXT. BEN GURION AIRPORT, TEL AVIV – DAY
scenes.summary: El-Al aircraft lands at Ben Gurion International Airport in Tel Aviv as Rita continues her search for surgical expertise.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVENUE, TEL AVIV - DAY
scenes.summary: Exterior of large modern white Hichilov Hospital building in Tel Aviv with prominent signage.

scenes.heading: INT. LEVITCH MEDICAL CENTER, WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita waits in modest hospital waiting room, observing other patients including bandaged soldier and teenager with wired jaw while overhearing Hebrew conversation from doctor's office.

scenes.heading: INT. LEVITCH MEDICAL CENTER, WASSERMAN’S OFFICE – DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Wasserman, inscrutable surgeon with buzz cut and blue eyes, reviews Rita's file. Rita explains she's found the right doctor - one who doesn't try to be nice or push his services.

scenes.heading: INT. LEVITCH MEDICAL CENTER, WASSERMAN’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita returns to stunned Dr. Wasserman's office. He asks to speak with her client, but Rita warns that to listen to Manitas is to accept the job.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHIHUAHUA DESERT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: SUV headlights cut through Mexican desert night as Dr. Wasserman complains about loud ranchera music. Rita asks the men to turn down the volume.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANITAS’ COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Five cars park in circle around Manitas' compound. Generator powers projectors creating lit circle as canvas shelter is erected. Wasserman observes the armed crowd with anthropologist's curiosity.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANITAS’ COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita introduces Manitas Del Monte to Professor Wasserman. After brief pleasantries, both men are ready to begin immediately and disappear into Manitas' armored vehicle.
scenes.turningPointNote: Surgeon and patient meet for gender transition consultation

scenes.heading: EXT. MANITAS’ COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Camp comes alive with music, drone surveillance, arriving women escorts, and provisions of water, beer and mezcal being unloaded. Rita accepts a beer and observes the festivities.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita watches people dance around campfire - sicarios, wives, children. Jessi Del Monte, Manitas' pretty 20-year-old wife, approaches and introduces herself, questioning Rita's purpose.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita meets Jessi, Manitas' wife who will be affected by his transformation

scenes.heading: EXT. COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks over desert compound. Smoke rises from dead campfire as sicarios sleep off their mezcal in pickup trucks after the party ends.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAMPEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita smokes cigarette watching sunrise when Wasserman gives her the burner phone and his red notebook. He warns the notebook's contents could condemn many to death if discovered.

scenes.heading: INT. MANITAS’ TRAILER
scenes.summary: In moving trailer, Rita hands Manitas three new passports and account information while he stares at his children's photos. She explains the coded transfers will make them rich as he begins singing.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUSANNE VILLA
scenes.summary: Moon hangs over Alps and Lake Geneva like steel mirror as title card establishes Switzerland location.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA LAUSANNE
scenes.summary: Two minibuses with open trunks in snow. Men carry Vuitton luggage and sleeping children Ángel and Diego to house while Jessi shivers and cries, wanting to return home.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - DAY
scenes.summary: In taxi to airport, Rita reports the family is safe. Manitas responds that everything must disappear and Rita is now his last link to the past.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Close-up of Wasserman's notebook burning, pages twisting and consumed by flames, destroying all evidence of the planned transformation.

scenes.heading: INT. JESSI’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: TV news reports DNA testing confirms human remains found in Veracruz warehouse belong to Manitas Del Monte, declaring the notorious drug trafficker dead at enemies' hands.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manitas officially declared dead, completing his disappearance

scenes.heading: INT. JESSI’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In blue TV light, Jessi watches news coverage showing photos of Manitas' criminal career - murdered rivals, severed heads, hanging corpses. She cries out in denial.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEL AVIV - DAY
scenes.summary: Sun rises over Tel Aviv as new chapter begins with instrumental music.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM HICHILOV HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Subjective camera as patient awakens in dark hospital room, breathing heavily. Wasserman enters from bright corridor. Eyes adjust slowly to sunlight through blinds.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM HICHILOV HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Patient uses small mirror to examine stomach, hyperventilating with delight at surgical results, groaning gratitude to God while TV plays weather report.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manitas awakens as Emilia, seeing her transformed body for first time

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM HICHILOV HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia sits on hospital bed trying on bras, body showing surgical traces. She practices her new voice saying 'Emilia Pérez' repeatedly. Title card: London, Four Years Later.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chic London restaurant with ten similar-looking wealthy diners. Rita, now polished executive, discusses business agreements, having become the successful woman she always wanted to be.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mexican woman approaches Rita at restaurant. They discover shared heritage - woman from Monterrey, Rita from Veracruz. Woman reveals she's been traveling long time, asks if Rita misses Mexico.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita unknowingly meets transformed Emilia in London restaurant

scenes.heading: INT. LAUSANNE VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: In Lausanne villa, blonde Jessi angrily refuses phone request to return to Mexico, asking who cared for her the last four years. She's comfortable with her wealth in Switzerland.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUSANNE VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Minibuses load Vuitton luggage as teenage Ángel and Diego, now blonde like their mother, climb in bickering. Rita tells Jessi they'll live with Emilia Pérez in Colonia Roma mansion.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Aerial view of sprawling, polluted Mexico City with distant 'Se compran' chant echoing from the opening scene.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA'S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Tighter aerial shot reveals magnificent modern villa on hillside with lush greenery. Voices announce the family's arrival as car doors slam.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA'S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: In spacious villa, Emilia takes Jessi's face in her hands, claiming they've never met. She declares herself like a sister, welcoming Jessi and children home. Both women embrace crying.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia reunites with her former wife Jessi, who doesn't recognize her

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita puts on coat to leave as Emilia quietly asks her assessment of Jessi. Rita notes Jessi seems lost and needs care, surprised Emilia doesn't know her better.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks over villa grounds with fine weather and irrigation hoses watering the lush landscape.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Three maids prepare breakfast as children eat cereal silently, avoiding eye contact with Emilia. She tries engaging them about school, learning their mother doesn't work and they wonder about attending school in Mexico.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: A maid carries a tray upstairs and walks down the corridor in Emilia's villa during the day.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA JESSI’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jessi wakes up in her room and sings 'Bienvenida' about being trapped in a luxurious prison. Dark thought-like figures appear, contrasting with her song as she reflects on her captive situation.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita and Emilia visit a market where Rita reconnects with familiar shop owners. Emilia links arms with Rita and offers to pay her to stay, suggesting she do nothing but just be there.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia's growing dependence on Rita becomes explicit

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia returns home exhausted at night and drops a photo of a young disappeared person on a table before heading upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia checks on the children. Ángel tells her she smells like his father, triggering a musical duet about memory and loss as Emilia struggles with her dual identity.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia's car arrives at Central Prison where a chaplain gets in to speak with her privately, discussing something behind closed windows.

scenes.heading: INT. CENTRAL PRISON/CONFESSIONAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside the prison confessional, a skinny sicario El Flaco confesses to the chaplain about killing hostages, while examining a photo of a young man.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRISON, PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: The priest leaves the prison and returns to the limousine where he and Emilia speak privately through the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED REFINERY, CIUDAD JUAREZ - DAY
scenes.summary: At an abandoned refinery in Ciudad Juarez, ten men dig and uncover human remains - clothes, limbs, and skulls tangled in earth and roots.

scenes.heading: INT. HANGAR - DAY
scenes.summary: In a hangar, Emilia embraces a grieving woman among ten body bags. She sings 'La Lucecita' about feeling love for herself for the first time when the woman's tears touched her hands.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia's transformation into a force for good begins

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia and Rita face reporters announcing La Lucecita NGO. They field questions about funding and methods, with Emilia saying she'll finance it herself.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA HEADQUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: At La Lucecita headquarters, Rita worries about media attention and investigation. Emilia dismisses concerns, suggesting they can buy off journalists as usual.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA HEADQUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Women of all ages wait at La Lucecita offices, singing 'Para' about needing to know what happened to their disappeared loved ones and being able to properly mourn them.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Through the villa's bay windows, Emilia's silhouette is seen dancing alone against the lights of Mexico City at night.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA EMILIA - NUIT
scenes.summary: Jessi returns home at night, kissing someone goodbye in a car. She walks up the gravel path, removes her shoes, and continues smiling.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA'S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia continues dancing when Jessi enters. They drink together as Emilia questions Jessi about her marriage to Manitas, learning Jessi was young and unsure if he truly loved her.

scenes.heading: INT. FUNDRAISER GALA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a fundraising gala, Emilia handles reporter questions about La Lucecita's methods of using repentant sicarios to find remains.

scenes.heading: INT. FUNDRAISING GALA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During Emilia's gala speech to ministers and officials, Rita sings 'El Mal' about the corruption among the audience, naming specific crimes while no one seems to hear her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita's growing awareness of the moral complexity of their situation

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia arrives at La Lucecita offices in the morning, responding distractedly to greetings from staff members.

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA TOILETS - DAY
scenes.summary: A well-dressed woman in her forties takes a sheathed knife from her bag in the La Lucecita restroom, examining it before putting it back.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The same woman returns to her seat in the La Lucecita waiting room, concealing her weapon.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA EMILIA’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Epifanía Flores meets with Emilia about her found husband. When told he's dead in the morgue, she reacts with apparent relief rather than grief.

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia and Epifanía part ways in the corridor, with Emilia calling after her as she's about to leave.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia asks what to do with Epifanía's husband's body. Epifanía says to throw it away. Both women reveal their concealed weapons and smile, with Emilia asking to see her again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Beginning of Emilia's romantic relationship with Epifanía

scenes.heading: INT. EPIFANÍA’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Early morning in Epifanía's modest home. Emilia makes coffee and feels at home in the cozy, ordered disorder. They kiss as Emilia prepares to leave before the children wake.

scenes.heading: INT. EPIFANÍA’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia sings 'El Amor' about her fragmented identity - half he/half she, half rich/half poor - while Epifanía goes about her morning routine, weighed down by love.

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA OFFICES - DAY
scenes.summary: At La Lucecita offices, Rita admires Emilia's impact on people's lives, contrasting it with her own previous work helping the corrupt get richer.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita walks alone through busy night streets, knowing where she's going, and pushes through the door of a night bar.

scenes.heading: INT. NIGHTCLUB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a dark nightclub, Rita flirts with a man at the bar. She discovers Jessi in another room with thin man Gustavo Brun and others, partying and drinking.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia has breakfast with the children in a cheerful atmosphere. She sees Jessi returning from an all-night party and signals her to be discreet about the children.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia accompanies the children to their driver, then goes upstairs to find Jessi. The corridor to Jessi's room seems unusually long.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA JESSI’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia confronts Jessi about her behavior, asking her to be careful around the children. Jessi suddenly announces she's getting married to Gustavo.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jessi's decision threatens Emilia's family structure

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two men beat up Gustavo Brun on the street. After he stops moving, they give him $100,000 and threaten him to leave the city and never return.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia discovers Jessi has left with the children, taking all their belongings. A three-way musical conversation begins between Emilia, Rita, and Jessi as they each process the crisis.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia's worst fear realized - losing her children

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA OFFICES - EVENING
scenes.summary: At La Lucecita offices, Rita and Epifanía wait for Emilia who has missed appointments. They bond over their connection to the missing woman.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: A poorly dressed young boy on an oversized bike navigates through Mexico City traffic, carrying a package on a string.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: The boy delivers a mysterious package to Rita at the La Lucecita building, where the atmosphere is funereal with people weeping and consoling each other.

scenes.heading: INT. RITA’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita unwraps the blood-soaked package in her office, discovering five severed fingers with Emilia's nail polish. She vomits and receives a threatening phone call from Gustavo.
scenes.turningPointNote: Confirmation that Emilia has been captured and tortured

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita meets with El Ponchis and armed men in the La Lucecita building at night, planning what appears to be a rescue operation.

scenes.heading: INT. RITA’S CAR - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita drives expressionlessly with two bags and a crackling walkie-talkie on her dashboard, heading to the exchange.

scenes.heading: EXT. RITA’S CAR + COUNTRY ROAD - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita's car leads a convoy of armed men in pickups along a deserted road before they split up strategically for the rescue operation.

scenes.heading: INT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN, KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In Gustavo's cabin, Emilia lies wounded under a bloody sheet with bandaged hands. Jessi gives her water and reveals they want to recover stolen money before killing her.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: La Ponchis and her men take position behind rocks overlooking the cabin as Rita's pickup arrives. Rita arms herself and prepares for the exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustavo puts on night vision goggles and orders the lights turned off, watching Rita's arrival through the green-tinted view.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two shots are fired. Rita throws herself to the ground as the headlights go off. She sees La Ponchis giving strategic hand signals.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN + LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Through night vision, Gustavo directs Rita's movements and orders his men to bring Emilia out while others provide cover.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita takes cover behind her pickup and observes the battlefield. Smoke from weapons fire begins flowing in reverse, returning to the cabin in an unnatural way.

scenes.heading: INT. HACIENDA, KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The supernatural smoke enters Emilia's mouth, awakening her. She reveals to Jessi that she is Manitas, her husband, confessing how she fell in love with Jessi through her sister years ago.
scenes.turningPointNote: The ultimate revelation of Emilia's true identity to Jessi

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustavo Brun's car speeds through the night in a chaotic chase, zigzagging as gunshots flash through the interior, before suddenly swerving off the road and plunging down an embankment into a ravine.
scenes.turningPointNote: The violent climax reaches its peak as Gustavo's car crashes, marking the physical culmination of the cartel violence that has driven the entire narrative

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The aftermath reveals Gustavo's car crashed a hundred meters below in the ravine, setting up the finality of the violent sequence.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The camera focuses on the wrecked car's trunk as ominous sounds of twisted metal and groaning fill the air, before the vehicle suddenly explodes in flames.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOUNTAIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita stands powerless above the ravine, watching the blazing car burn below, the red flames reflecting on her face as she witnesses the violent end.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita's moment of powerless witnessing represents her ultimate confrontation with the consequences of the violent world she became entangled in

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: At Emilia's house, Rita dressed in black mourning watches from a window as Diego and Ángel arrive escorted by police, while Epifanía begins reciting a poem dedicated to the beloved deceased.
scenes.turningPointNote: The shift to mourning and the children's arrival under police escort marks the transition from violence to its emotional aftermath and consequences

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY SUBURBS - DAY
scenes.summary: Epifanía leads a funeral procession through Mexico City suburbs, singing about one who changed lead to gold and raised the flag of truth, as mourners carry a naive statue of Emilia showing her missing fingers.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia's transformation into a folk saint figure represents the mythologizing of her complex legacy, completing her journey from cartel leader to martyred symbol
